sourceMovie.title: Arlington Road
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Arlington Road (1999) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Mark Pellington's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Kiss the Girls
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Arlington Road clicked for you, Kiss the Girls (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Arlington Road?
faq.answer: Try Kiss the Girls, Edge of Darkness, Gorky Park—each shares DNA with Arlington Road while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Arlington Road?
faq.answer: Arlington Road (1999) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Arlington Road worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
